title: Specialization · Computer Vision (deep dive)
focus: CV-specific architectures, augmentation, OD/segmentation, modern foundation models.

items.label: CNN families: ResNet → EfficientNet → ConvNeXt
interviewQuestions: Compare ResNet, EfficientNet, and ConvNeXt — when would you reach for each?
Why did ConvNeXt show CNNs can still match transformers?
What's the role of compound scaling in EfficientNet?

items.label: Vision Transformer (ViT) paper
interviewQuestions: When does a ViT beat a CNN, and when doesn't it?
How does the patch embedding work in a ViT?
Why did ViTs need so much pretraining data to match CNNs?

items.label: CLIP — image-text contrastive pretraining
interviewQuestions: How does CLIP enable zero-shot image classification?
Walk me through CLIP's contrastive training objective.

items.label: Object detection: YOLO / DETR / SAM
interviewQuestions: Compare YOLO, DETR, and SAM — when does each fit?
Why is non-max suppression still the standard post-processing for OD?
How would you handle small-object detection in a custom dataset?

items.label: Image augmentation patterns (RandAugment, CutMix, MixUp)
interviewQuestions: Compare RandAugment, CutMix, and MixUp — when each helps.
Why does augmentation help less on very large datasets?
What augmentation strategy would you pick for a small medical-imaging dataset?

items.label: Design a content-moderation system for user-uploaded images
Fine-tune CLIP / ResNet on a small custom dataset

interviewQuestions: When does a ViT beat a CNN, and when doesn't it?
How does CLIP enable zero-shot image classification?
Walk me through how you'd build an image moderation system end-to-end.
What augmentation strategy would you pick for a small medical-imaging dataset?

references.label: fast.ai — Practical Deep Learning for Coders
Dive into Deep Learning (d2l.ai) — interactive book
